mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains above 100k signal robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly gains.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 0.5% indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 0.5% monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth above 3% signals robust economic demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of quarters where GDPC1 exceeds 3% annualized growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and unemployment rate signal accommodative monetary policy and a strong labor market, creating a positive feedback loop for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls > 0.25pp and UNRATE falls > 0.1pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPI > 3%) and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals a monetary tightening cycle that pressures equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in residential sales (RSAFS) below 680k signals weak housing demand, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS falls below 680k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in unemployment rate above 0.2pp signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp.
